title: lepicephalus succisa
intro: Lepicephalus succisa, also known as the "greater knapweed" or "hardheads," is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Lepicephalus succisa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
